untranslatable_reason: This signal requires multi-asset relative strength comparison (Asset A vs Asset B outperformance) and implied volatility ranking across two different instruments. The allowed indicator set contains only single-asset technical indicators (RSI, ATR, MACD, etc.) and no cross-asset comparison operators, relative strength metrics, or implied volatility surface data. The signal logic depends on: (1) directional conviction in relative strength between two assets, (2) comparing implied volatility levels across two different instruments, and (3) volatility spread dynamics. None of these can be expressed with the available single-asset indicators and operators.
translation_notes: This is a volatility arbitrage/relative value strategy from Natenberg's options theory. It requires: (1) IV_B > IV_A comparison (implied volatility data not available), (2) relative strength conviction between two assets (cross-asset metric not available), (3) volatility spread monitoring (requires multi-leg volatility data). The strategy is not translatable to a single-asset price/volume/technical indicator framework. Consider: adding IV surface indicators, cross-asset return comparisons, or relative performance metrics to the allowed indicator list.
